creator: cem@freebsd.org
text:
I think the mechanism by which we fault here (amd64 and other 64-bit time_t platforms) is:

days (int) is overflowed to be negative by:

  days = secs / SECDAY;  // (secs is time_t==int64_t on amd64)

ct->day (int) inherits a negative value from days.

In atrtc_set(), we invoke bin2bcd(ct.day) => bin2bcd_data[ct.day] (valid indices are 0-99) => dereferencing 2GB before the array.

It seems like ct->day being negative (or more specifically, outside 0-31) is a bug.  It seems like several places here could have better assertions to protect against invalid ranges.
